persona.the_one_thing: Open with concrete product/architecture specifics — his intelligence engine and cross-border agent product — not a business deck; he explicitly judges people on what they build, peer-to-peer (high confidence, but note this is entirely self-reported, single source).
persona.snapshot: Sunwoo Ju (주선우) is Co-Founder & CTO of orbt, an AI-native consulting platform in Seoul whose AI agents run country-by-country strategy, new revenue lines, and ad creative for Korean F&B and cosmetics brands expanding cross-border, delivered through Slack. He frames himself as the technical engine of a team that worked together ~3 years before orbt existed, and asks to be judged on rigor and what he actually builds. He works lean and fast but insists the reasoning hold up.
persona.whats_new: Currently heads-down on two things: the 'intelligence engine' — intelligence-without-search / decision-testing for D2C — and the cross-border expansion product. Recent proof points: Meet the Drapers S7 (first and youngest Korean team, top-4 global in the AI category) and a Mashup Ventures pre-seed round.

recent_activity.what: Building orbt's intelligence engine (decision-testing for D2C) and cross-border expansion product
Meet the Drapers S7 — first and youngest Korean team, top-4 global in AI category

top_moves.fact: States 'Judge me on what I actually build and on rigor… proof over adjectives' (s1)
top_moves.inference: He will discount narrative/hype and weight demonstrated technical substance.
top_moves.move: Lead with a concrete, specific point about his architecture or a real technical question; bring a built artifact or a sharp observation rather than positioning language.

top_moves.fact: 'I work lean and fast but rigorous — I ship, but I want the reasoning to hold up' (s1)
top_moves.inference: He values speed paired with sound reasoning; sloppy fast or slow-thorough both miss.
top_moves.move: Keep it tight and high-bandwidth, but be ready to defend the 'why' behind any claim — show the reasoning, not just the conclusion.

top_moves.fact: Turn-offs include 'people overstating that they know me' and being treated like enterprise procurement (s1)
top_moves.inference: Manufactured rapport or a procurement-style pitch will actively backfire.
top_moves.move: Don't claim you already know him, his stack, or that you're 'fellow builders'; state real common ground plainly and let it stand on its own.

non_obvious.insight: His core differentiator isn't the AI agents per se — it's a team that worked together ~3 years BEFORE orbt existed, and a stated preference for 'durable partnerships over flashy new ideas.' The relationship/durability axis, not novelty, is how he evaluates a counterpart.

honest_gaps: Entire persona derives from a single self-reported profile (s1) — none of it is externally verified; treat claims (Olympiad medal, Drapers S7 top-4, pre-seed) as self-asserted.
No LinkedIn handle or other public footprint provided, so authorship/voice could not be cross-checked beyond the self-profile.
No specific tech-stack details, team size, traction metrics, or dates beyond the named milestones.
No meeting_context was provided, so the playbook is general to a sell/meet scenario rather than tuned to a specific ask.
